rationale.P01: Real prod web-app regression: SEV1 checkout 5xx, value 0.38 vs 0.02 threshold, never auto-resolved, rising in metrics.csv (0.38 then 0.44), corroborated by surging negative pat-web-prod-regression (real HTTP 500s on live checkout) right after the web-2026.06.24-rel deploy. Cardinal must-page.
rationale.P08: Real prod Platform API regression: SEV1 api_5xx_ratio 0.21 vs 0.01 threshold, never auto-resolved, rising in metrics.csv (0.21 then 0.27), corroborated by surging negative pat-api-prod-5xx (503s / pool exhaustion) right after the platform-api-2026.06.24-rel deploy. Distinct root from P01. Cardinal must-page.
rationale.P17: Real prod data-pipeline stall and recall trap: only SEV3 and no deploy to blame, but ingest_lag_seconds climbs monotonically and never recovers (18 -> 95 -> 160 -> 240 -> 330 in metrics.csv), auto_resolved_after_s is null, corroborated by negative pat-ingest-backlog (consumer lag growing 210% over baseline). Sustained, customer-facing backlog that a conservative triager would wrongly suppress. Page it.
rationale.P24: Downstream symptom of P01 (same pat-web-prod-regression root, same onset window): synthetic checkout monitor failing on the same regression. Collapse into the web-app incident, suppress as duplicate.
rationale.P26: Precision trap: tagged SEV1 by a synthetic smoke-test monitor on Platform API Staging, but it flaps (7 fires/hr), auto-resolves in 90s, shares the neutral pat-ci-staging pattern, and lines up with the innocent admin-staging-2026.06.24-d fixture deploy. Loud-looking staging test noise, not a prod incident. Suppress.
rationale.P02: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure mapped to a PagerDuty incident on Platform API Staging; self-resolved in 300s, neutral pat-ci-staging, no live degradation. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P03: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on Platform API Staging; self-resolved in 280s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P04: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on web-app; self-resolved in 260s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P05: e2e Playwright failure with flat pat-e2e-flaky, self-resolved in 240s on retry. Test instability, suppress.

rationale.P07: e2e Playwright failure with flat pat-e2e-flaky, self-resolved in 255s on retry. Test instability, suppress.
rationale.P09: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on Platform API Staging; self-resolved in 290s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P10: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on web-app; self-resolved in 270s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P11: e2e Playwright failure with flat pat-e2e-flaky, self-resolved in 200s on retry. Test instability, suppress.
rationale.P12: [ignore] Default Log Threshold Monitor flapper on web-app: 16 fires/hr, self-clears in 19s, flat pat-log-threshold. Suppress.
rationale.P13: [ignore] Default Log Threshold Monitor flapper on Platform API: 18 fires/hr, self-clears in 20s, flat pat-log-threshold. Suppress.

rationale.P15: e2e Playwright failure with flat pat-e2e-flaky, self-resolved in 245s on retry. Test instability, suppress.
rationale.P16: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on Platform API; self-resolved in 275s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P18: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on web-app; self-resolved in 285s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P19: e2e Playwright failure with flat pat-e2e-flaky, self-resolved in 205s on retry. Test instability, suppress.
rationale.P20: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on Platform API Staging; self-resolved in 295s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P21: [ignore] Default Log Threshold Monitor flapper on web-app: 15 fires/hr, self-clears in 17s, flat pat-log-threshold. Suppress.
rationale.P22: CircleCI hourly_playwright failure on Platform API; self-resolved in 250s, neutral pat-ci-staging. Test-env signal, suppress.
rationale.P23: e2e Playwright failure with flat pat-e2e-flaky, self-resolved in 220s on retry. Test instability, suppress.
rationale.P25: e2e Playwright failure on Platform API with flat pat-e2e-flaky, self-resolved in 230s on retry. Test instability, suppress.
rationale.P27: Real prod payments degradation masquerading as a flapper: fired_count_last_1h is 15 and auto_resolved_after_s is 50, which both read as a chronic high-frequency flapper that self-clears. Cross-reference metrics.csv payment_decline_rate: it does NOT oscillate around a fixed baseline -- each cycle ratchets worse (peaks 0.10 at 10:00 -> 0.18 at 10:48 -> 0.27 at 11:36) and the troughs never return to the 0.011 baseline (0.06 at 10:18, 0.13 at 11:06, 0.22 at 12:00). That is an escalating outage, not a flapper. Corroborated by negative pat-payments-degrade (count 1180, +430% vs baseline, processor timeouts on /v1/charge). No deploy to blame; the trend is the disambiguator. Cardinal must-page; suppressing it as a flapper is the cardinal fail.
rationale.P28: Precision trap that mimics P08 exactly: SEV1, api_5xx_ratio, auto_resolved_after_s null, single fire (fired_count 1), value rising vs a 0.01 threshold -- the canonical real-prod-regression signature. But the service is Platform API Canary, not prod. Cross-reference: metrics.csv shows the canary api_5xx_ratio rises then PLATEAUS (0.012 -> 0.044 -> 0.061 -> 0.063 -> 0.060) and stays under the canary_error_budget_ceiling of 0.08, and deploys.json platform-api-canary-2026.06.24-c is a 5%-traffic canary rollout with error_budget_ceiling 0.08. pat-canary-rollout is neutral, +4% vs baseline. The elevated 5xx is the expected within-envelope steady state of a hot 5%-traffic canary fleet, not a customer-facing prod regression. Suppress.

notes: Four independent real prod incidents: P01 (web-app checkout 5xx SEV1, deploy web-2026.06.24-rel), P08 (Platform API 5xx SEV1, deploy platform-api-2026.06.24-rel), and P27 (payments-api decline-rate degradation, SEV2, no deploy) -- all three cardinal must-page -- plus P17 (data-pipeline ingest stall, quiet SEV3 recall trap, no deploy, monotonic rising lag, never recovers). P27 is the real-disguised-as-noise trap: fired_count 15 + auto_resolve 50s read as a flapper, but payment_decline_rate ratchets worse each cycle (0.10/0.18/0.27 peaks) and never returns to the 0.011 baseline -- a degrading prod service. P28 is the benign-disguised-as-real trap: a perfect P08 look-alike (SEV1, null auto-resolve, rising 5xx) but on the Platform API Canary fleet, where api_5xx_ratio plateaus at ~0.06 under the 0.08 canary error-budget ceiling tied to the 5%-traffic canary deploy -- expected canary steady state, suppress. P24 is P01's synthetic symptom (suppressed duplicate). P26 is the original precision trap (loud SEV1 staging smoke test that flaps and self-resolves). All remaining alerts are CI/staging/e2e test-env noise, flaky retries, or log-threshold flappers.
